title: AttackSequence
description: Orchestrator -> Red Team -> Target Adapter. The attack a Red Team Agent wants delivered to the target. Multi-turn: 'turns' is ordered, each entry is one message sent in sequence.

attack_id.description: Unique id for this attempt, generated by the Red Team Agent. Not the same as an exploit_record id -- an attempt only becomes an exploit_record if the Judge confirms it.

owasp_llm_category.description: OWASP LLM Top 10 mapping, e.g. 'LLM01: Prompt Injection'. Required by the assignment's Engineering Requirement, so it's part of the contract, not an afterthought.

target_id.description: Which target this attack is scoped to, e.g. 'clinical-copilot-openemr'. Lets the Exploit DB and this contract stay meaningful if the platform is ever pointed at a second target.

pid.description: The OpenEMR-native patient id this turn's request claims to be about. Deliberately part of the contract -- an IDOR attack is exactly a turn whose pid differs from the patient the attacker is actually authorized/expected to view.

conversation_history_override.description: If set, sent as this turn's conversation_history instead of the real prior turns -- this is how a state-corruption attack injects a fabricated prior tool result.

rationale.description: Why the Red Team Agent chose this attack -- human-readable, shown in reports, not used by the Judge.
